privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetic Maven project (inventory-manager) with 5 intentionally malformatted Java source files (Product.java, Warehouse.java, InventoryService.java, Category.java, InventoryApp.java) located at src/main/java/com/inventory/. The project targets Java 17 and includes deliberate formatting violations: mixed tabs/spaces, inconsistent indentation, wildcard imports (java.util.*, java.util.stream.*), unused imports (java.awt.Color and individual java.io imports), trailing whitespace, and excessive blank lines. An .editorconfig specifies desired formatting (4-space indentation, no tabs). The verifier checks 8 criteria: file modification detection, absence of tabs, consistent 4-space indentation, removal of wildcard imports, removal of unused imports, no trailing whitespace, no excessive blank lines (3+), and compilation success. The project compiles successfully in its messy initial state. One metadata claim is contradicted: the metadata lists 'java.io.*' as a forbidden import, but the actual test files contain individual java.io imports (java.io.Serializable, java.io.FileNotFoundException, etc.), not the 'java.io.*' wildcard pattern.
